ddls:
CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "session" TEXT /* Session period (e.g., 2021-2025) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the student belongs to */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the student */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the student joined */,
  "is_sent" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the student's transaction has been sent */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the student's status is verified */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the student */,
  "enrollment_status" TEXT /* Current enrollment status of the student */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "head_of_department" TEXT /* Name of the head of the department */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the department */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the department */,
  "building_location" TEXT /* Physical location of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the student associated with the transaction */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., Membership, Fee Payment) */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date the transaction was made */,
  "is_sent" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the transaction has been sent */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the transaction is verified */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the transaction */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional details about the transaction */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment used for the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_transaction_type FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_type") REFERENCES transaction_types ("transaction_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transaction_receipts" (
  "receipt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each receipt */,
  "transaction_id" TEXT /* ID of the transaction the receipt belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the receipt */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the receipt file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the receipt was uploaded */,
  "receipt_number" TEXT /* Unique number for the receipt */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the receipt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("receipt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transaction_receipts_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transaction_receipts_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_images" (
  "image_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student the image belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the image */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the image file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the image was uploaded */,
  "image_type" TEXT /* Type of image (e.g., profile, ID card) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_images_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transaction_types" (
  "transaction_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction type */,
  "transaction_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the transaction type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transaction type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the transaction type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_methods" (
  "contact_method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact method */,
  "contact_method_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact method (e.g., Phone, Email) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the contact method */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the contact method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_contacts" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact method entry */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student */,
  "contact_method_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact method */,
  "contact_value" TEXT /* Value of the contact method (e.g., phone number, email) */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this is the primary contact method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_contacts_contact_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("contact_method_id") REFERENCES contact_methods ("contact_method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_contacts_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, staff, student) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student whose record was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
